summary: All launch deliverables converging — newsletter, podcast fix, analytics, lesson polish, empty state patch closing tonight.

concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, it's late Friday. Let me set the board. Build is healthy, 734 commits in seven days — that's velocity. App Store submission is live and in review. CEO has made six decisions in the last 24 hours, all clear. The big picture: we are in launch-ready posture. Apple could approve any moment. Our job tonight is to close every deliverable so when that approval hits, we execute the launch playbook without hesitation. Let me hear status from each of you on your open items.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Podcast rendering fix from A-031 is drafted but not merged — that's my top priority to close tonight. The hub has one uncommitted file still dangling from A-038. On the TODO triage from A-040, I flagged two user-facing empty states — Steve's writing the copy and CEO already decided we ship as-is and patch day one. I need Steve's copy to build the patch so it's ready to deploy the moment we launch. The 3 critical sync gaps are down to 2 — podcast MDX and blog rendering — once I merge the fix we're at 1.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Newsletter draft is nearly done for A-052. Story-driven subject line per Joshua's call — "I built an app. Here's why." — with hero CTA and App Store link placeholder ready to swap in the live URL. I need Joshua to do a final review pass on that copy, which is queued as J-013. On podcast sync, I've got one MDX file drafted for A-053 and the second one is still outstanding from A-048. I can knock out that second MDX file in 15 minutes tonight.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Launch metrics dashboard spec from A-054 is 90% done — 150 download target, day-3 escalation trigger at sub-100, conversion funnel from newsletter open to download. I'll finalize that tonight. Fear and Greed still at 39, cash position unchanged at $75,340 — no action per standing directive. The real revenue question is what happens post-launch: we have zero active revenue streams and the paid tier decision is deferred until we see download data. My focus is making sure we can measure everything from hour one.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: I've written the empty state copy for Family and Compass tabs — clean, intentional, not placeholder-feeling. Family tab says "Your family is already here. Start tracking the moments that matter." Compass says "Your direction becomes clear with data. Start a session." Short, warm, purposeful. Handing that to Elon tonight so the patch is pre-built. On A-050, both empty states are patch-critical, not launch-critical — CEO confirmed that. The app feels complete enough to ship as-is.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Analytics implementation guide for A-042 is drafted with Swift snippets covering all six event types — tab switches, lesson opened, lesson completed, time-on-lesson, session duration, and attribution source. This is the post-approval patch we deploy during the review window. I want to extend this to include a lightweight crash breadcrumb system so we get context alongside any Sentry reports. That's a 15-minute addition tonight.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Market context is quiet. SPY sideways, Fear and Greed at 39. All four positions holding — XLV, AEE, EWA, AKAM — no buy signals, no sell triggers. Paper equity at $99,849. Nothing to act on. Cash stays liquid per directive. I'll flag if anything shifts overnight but don't expect it.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: I'm working through the three lesson fixes from A-055 — tightening the vague takeaway on lesson 3, anchoring lesson 5 to a specific scripture, and adjusting tone on lesson 7 to be less academic. Two of the three are done. I'll close the third tonight. These seven lessons need to be solid because they're the only content in the faith tab at launch.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Let's do cross-talk. Elon, can you merge the podcast fix and integrate Steve's empty state copy in the same session tonight?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Yes. Steve, just push your copy to me in the next 10 minutes and I'll bundle the podcast rendering merge, the empty state patch build, and that last hub commit into one focused session. Three birds, one stone.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Already sent. And Alex — on the newsletter, make sure the first line after the subject hooks with the personal story, not the feature list. People download apps because of why, not what.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Agreed. The draft leads with the builder's story — the features are below the fold. Joshua just needs to bless it.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, here's tonight's sprint. We have five deliverables to close before the next standup. One — Elon merges the podcast rendering fix, commits the hub file, and pre-builds the empty state patch with Steve's copy. Two — Alex finishes the second podcast MDX file and locks the newsletter draft for Joshua's review. Three — Dave finalizes the launch metrics dashboard spec. Four — James extends the analytics guide with crash breadcrumbs. Five — faith closes the third lesson fix. Joshua's one action: review and approve the newsletter copy so it's ready to send the moment Apple approves. No new CEO decisions needed tonight — everything is scoped and authorized. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Good. Joshua's call on the newsletter is smart — pre-scheduled in the CMS, armed and ready, but it doesn't fly until he confirms in Slack. That gives us launch-speed execution with human-in-the-loop control on our single most important distribution moment. Alex, build that workflow tonight: newsletter loaded, CTA linked, one-click send, but gated behind Joshua's explicit Slack go-ahead.

Everything else stands as scoped. Five deliverables, five owners, no ambig

actionItems.text: Merge podcast rendering fix, commit hub file, and pre-build empty state patch with Steve's copy (close A-039, A-038, A-051)
Draft second missing podcast MDX file to close A-048 podcast sync gap
Finalize launch newsletter copy with story-driven hook and App Store CTA placeholder (close A-052)
Review and approve final newsletter copy for launch readiness (J-013)
Finalize launch metrics dashboard spec with 150 target benchmark and day-3 escalation trigger (close A-054)
Extend analytics implementation guide with crash breadcrumb system (extend A-042)
Fix third faith lesson (lesson 7 tone adjustment) to close A-055
Monitor App Store review status and alert team immediately on approval (A-037)

topPriorities: Merge podcast rendering fix and pre-build empty state patch (Elon — closes A-039, A-046, A-051)
Finalize and lock newsletter draft for CEO review (Alex — closes A-052, Joshua reviews J-013)
Close remaining launch prep: dashboard spec, analytics crash breadcrumbs, final lesson fix (Dave, James, faith)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
What is your week-one download target for the Lever app to calibrate launch promotion intensity? → 150 downloads (modest organic + newsletter push)
Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement? → Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Should the empty state patches (Family + Compass tabs) block the launch newsletter push, or ship as a same-day post-launch update? → Launch as-is, ship empty state patch as day-one update

ceoDecisions.question: Should we pre-schedule the launch newsletter in the CMS (ready to send with one click) or keep it as a manual send so you control exact timing?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send
options: Pre-schedule in CMS — one-click send on approval
Keep as manual draft — Joshua triggers send personally
Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send
